title: [CI:BUILD] copr: fix el8 build
body:
This common set seemed to work for all envs:
- disable debuginfo
- GO111MODULE=on

TODO: enable GO111MODULE=off
TODO: enable debuginfo

Fedora 35 builds are disabled, so remove fedora 35
conditionals while we're at it.

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

Successful el8 build with this change: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/5082067/

@mtrmac @vrothberg @rhatdan PTAL

comments.body: older builds were failing for el8 https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/5072894/

> Didn't we fix that already?

was fixed for podman, but not for skopeo (also buildah)

> *shrug* I’m fine with backports to older releases having to use a big hammer like this from time to time, but it’s not yet clear to me that this situation calls for that.
> 
> Was this discussed elsewhere? I can’t quickly find the suggested Podman variant of this PR.

In the upstream `podman.spec.rpkg`, I didn't include any justification other than `enable rhel8 builds` as IIRC, I took it from the rhel podman spec file which had that addition at least in 2019.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
for whatever reason, the el8 environment doesn't seem to pick up the BUILDTAGS set in the spec file. Looks like the default %gobuild macro is messed up.
PR updated. Successful copr build at: https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/build/5389428/ . PTAL.

> Ah, thanks. That’s documented in an internal “Container Tools build process for RHEL/OCP” document.

Ack, totally forgot about that doc :D .
> 
> LGTM, this is clearly an improvement.

Thanks.

> 
> WRT the debug info, it might be worth trying to make the `gobuild` macro much closer to the RHEL8 version in `go-srpm-macros` . Things like `-compressdwarf=false` seems suggestive (but to be clear, I have absolutely no idea what the effect is and whether it actually matters.)

Ack, I've found gobuild macro to be a moving target. I'll send a follow-up PR (hopefully soon). Thanks!
